title: • Refine ideas together (what’s working, what’s missing).
content:
• Refine ideas together (what’s working, what’s missing).
• Keep momentum going without pressure.

How This Works
1. Derek sends an email with:
• A rough idea he’s exploring.
• Any struggles he’s having (e.g., “I don’t know what happens next” or “My character feels flat”).
• A question or prompt for feedback.
2. I’ll reply with:
• Possible directions, clarifications, or questions to deepen the idea.
• A challenge (e.g., “Write this scene from a different POV”).
• Encouragement to stay in the exploratory phase—don’t overthink yet.
3. He responds with a revision or next step.
4. We repeat as needed until he feels confident in his direction.

This keeps things low-pressure, idea-driven, and momentum-based.

Final Takeaways

✅ Stories are about change. If a character isn’t making choices, they’re stuck.
✅ Scenes must shift. Every scene should move from one state to another.
context: From document: Essential Storytelling Principles for Derek — chunk 4 (chunk 4/5)
